industries.name: Nuclear Applications & Space Power
uses: Neptunium-237 (²³⁷Np, t½ = 2.14 million years) is the primary long-lived transuranic waste component in spent nuclear fuel after uranium and plutonium — transmutation of ²³⁷Np in fast neutron reactors is studied as a strategy to reduce nuclear waste radiotoxic lifetime from millions of years to hundreds

²³⁷Np irradiated in a nuclear reactor produces plutonium-238 via neutron capture and beta decay (²³⁷Np + n → ²³⁸Np → ²³⁸Pu) — ²³⁸Pu is the essential heat source in NASA's radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs) for deep space missions beyond the range of solar panels

ORNL produces ²³⁸Pu at Oak Ridge from ²³⁷Np targets irradiated in the HFIR reactor — restarted in 2015 after a 30-year gap to ensure supply for NASA's Dragonfly (Titan), Europa Clipper, and future outer solar system missions
Neptunium metal has a critical mass for nuclear fission (~60 kg), making ²³⁷Np a safeguarded nuclear material tracked under IAEA protocols
Neptunium is the first transuranic element — discovered in 1940 at UC Berkeley by Edwin McMillan and Philip Abelson by neutron bombardment of uranium-238 in a cyclotron

companies.name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory (DOE)
companies.use: Primary US handler and processor of neptunium-237, extracted from spent nuclear fuel reprocessing. Restated ²³⁸Pu production in 2015 by irradiating Np-237 oxide targets in the HFIR reactor — the sole US source of ²³⁸Pu for NASA deep space RTGs. Targets are processed in ORNL's hot cells to separate the Pu-238 product.

companies.name: RIAR (Research Institute of Atomic Reactors)
companies.use: Russian nuclear research center at Dimitrovgrad operating high-flux research reactors that irradiate neptunium targets for ²³⁸Pu production for Russian space power programs. Also conducts neptunium transmutation research in fast neutron reactors as part of the Russian closed nuclear fuel cycle program.

companies.name: NASA (Jet Propulsion Laboratory)
companies.use: Primary end-user of neptunium-derived ²³⁸Pu in its MMRTG (Multi-Mission RTG) and GPHS-RTG systems. The Perseverance rover, Dragonfly Titan rotorcraft, and future outer planet missions all depend on ORNL's Np-237 irradiation program to produce the ²³⁸Pu fuel for their power systems.
